mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of Breadth-First Search (BFS)?
mcqs.explanation: The time complexity of BFS is O(b^d), where b is the branching factor and d is the depth of the goal node.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of Depth-First Search (DFS)?
mcqs.explanation: The space complexity of DFS is O(d), where d is the depth of the goal node.

mcqs.question: What is the main difference between Uniform-Cost Search (UCS) and Depth-Limited Search (DLS)?

options.text: UCS explores nodes with the lowest cost first, while DLS explores nodes up to a certain depth limit.
UCS explores nodes up to a certain depth limit, while DLS explores nodes with the lowest cost first.
UCS uses a priority queue, while DLS uses a stack.
UCS uses a stack, while DLS uses a priority queue.

mcqs.explanation: The main difference between UCS and DLS is that UCS explores nodes with the lowest cost first, while DLS explores nodes up to a certain depth limit.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of Iterative Deepening Depth-First Search (IDDFS)?
mcqs.explanation: The time complexity of IDDFS is O(b^d), where b is the branching factor and d is the depth of the goal node.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of using IDDFS over other search strategies?

options.text: IDDFS is guaranteed to find the optimal solution.
IDDFS is faster than other search strategies.
IDDFS uses less memory than other search strategies.
IDDFS is more efficient than other search strategies.

mcqs.explanation: The main advantage of using IDDFS is that it is guaranteed to find the optimal solution, unlike other search strategies that may get stuck in an infinite loop.
